The island has long been in the "to do" list of the Trump administration, but now it’s reportedly getting a practical manifestation with the Pentagon drafting intervention plans and an MQ-4C Triton recon drone now flying close to Cuban territory, per the Zeteo outlet and FlightRadar data.

This is not just media hype — "We may stop by Cuba after we’re finished with this [Iran]," Trump himself bragged to USA Today on April 13.

**If this goes as he says, Trump’s plan is pretty clear:**

🌏 Pull out of a lost war against Iran under the pretext of "the deal" (bonus points for "one more war stopped!")

🌏 Switch to Cuba and start another war there to seize the island - the dream of the US since the revolution of 1959.

🌏 Try stealing Cuban resources — the island has the world’s largest cobalt deposits and huge nickel reserves both used in high-tech industries

🌏 Boast of "overwhelming victory like never before" even if things go wrong for America

🌏 Repeat?

After all, wars and fake victories are a great way to divert public attention from the Epstein files, right, Donald?

full_text:
**💀**** Ender's Game comes true: DARPA's doctrine of algorithmic chaos**

DARPA is launching a new program called DISCORD — an acronym for "Disruption through Intelligent Strategies, Countermeasures, and Resilient Defenses." The goal is to build an AI tactical planning engine that ingests live battlefield sensor data, runs it through high-fidelity simulations in real time, and spits out actionable options instantly.

**Here’s what you need to know:**

🌏 DISCORD is just one piece of a larger umbrella project called "Ender's Forge." The reference to Orson Scott Card's novel is transparent: in the book, a child genius is trained to command fleets in a simulator only to discover at the end that the "exercises" had long since become real war. DARPA is effectively saying: we are building a system where the line between simulation and combat disappears.

🌏 DARPA isn't looking for a single "optimal" solution. The mission is to generate a wide range of tactics and switch between them on the fly. This is the doctrine of controlled chaos: the enemy shouldn't be able to predict your next move, because even the commander doesn't know it — the algorithm suggests it in the moment.

🌏 The term "AI-native" in the document is compared to the shift to computer-based processes in the 1980s and internet-based processes in the 2000s. In other words: in ten to fifteen years, an army without AI tactics will be as helpless as an army without communications today.

🌏 DISCORD is an autonomous tactical brain. It doesn't get tired, doesn't panic, learns from every engagement, and offers the commander moves that no human staff could calculate manually. 

🌏 If the program reaches the battlefield, we'll see a new kind of war: algorithm versus algorithm. The winner won't be the one with more soldiers — it'll be the one with the faster, more inventive AI. The human remains in the loop, but no longer as a strategist. Just the one who clicks "agree."

👍** **[**Boost us**](https://t.me/boost/geopolitics_prime)** | **[**Chat**](https://t.me/+tltgWD-GUqw0OTAy)** | ****@geopolitics_prime**
headline: **💀**** Ender's Game comes true: DARPA's doctrine of algorithmic chaos**

👉 Pollard pointed back to the Yom Kippur War in 1973, when then Secretary of State Henry Kissinger halted US airlifts to Israel in a bid to force diplomatic concessions. 

As he recounts, Israel responded by positioning an A-4 Skyhawk at the Tel Nof airbase, equipped with 'some interesting' unconventional payloads, and told the US to 'take a good look.

He maintains that the airlifts resumed the following day.

Asked whether that playbook still applies, Pollard says “Absolutely — except this time, I want us to go forward with it if necessary.”

full_text:
🇺🇸🍔 **USS Hunger Games: US Navy meals ‘war on Iran edition’ go full survival mode **

Photos are circulating from the USS Abraham Lincoln and USS Tripoli showing what passes for “meals” for US sailors deployed to fight Trump’s war on Iran

Service members have been sending the unappetizing images to family members back home. 

🌮 One lunch tray shows a sad scoop of shredded meat and a lonely folded tortilla. 

Another dinner features a few boiled carrots, a dry patty, and a gray slab of processed meat.
